pages.full_text:
It was a beautiful gilded church & felt a bit like an old Roman temple glade.

	On to __Malaucène__ beyons Vaison-la-Romaine. Les Bosquets ****,  where we were with William + Bryony 2 years ago. Lovely big L shaped pool. They have put 'portacabins, ugly, on the top level.
	We had lunch & swam & rested. We did a washing-machine load (at the snack bar) + put up the tent.
	In the evening we walked into Malaucène & ate at the restaurant with the fountain Blé aux Herbes. It rained a bit in the early morning.
__Thursday__
	At 9.30 we set off on a day trip to Vaison. We walked up the winding streets to the castle. Sheltered in a cafe during a shower. Lovely views of old parts of houses. The Roman Bridge didn't get washed away during the 1993 flood, although 37 people drowned, 150 houses were washed away & all the modern industrial estate. We also saw the Roman ruins ruins - 2 latrines & a rebuilt amphi-theatre. We bought chocolate puddings for this evening & baguettes for lunch. The cathedral has a lovely altar stone with fluted carvings JJJJJJJJJ

[margin, rotated:] Roman Bridge [CAD] over Ouvèze
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence; this is a continuation from the previous spread. 'beyons' likely 'beyond' (diarist's spelling). The marginal text rotated vertically on the right edge reads 'Roman Bridge [CAD] over Ouvèze' — 'CAD' could be an abbreviation or partial word, possibly 'C.AD' indicating century AD. The 1993 flood reference is a past event being described; year of diary entry cannot be determined from this page alone — year is post-1993. Campsite 'Les Bosquets' four-star. The fluted carvings notation 'JJJJJJJJJ' appears to be decorative scallop/wave symbols drawn by the diarist. 'Blé aux Herbes' is the name of the restaurant.

pages.full_text:
& stone apse & there's a pretty cloisters.
There was a hotel de la Beffroy, Beffroi, what is the origin of [the word?]

[photo with caption:] Roman Bridge [CAD] over Ouvèze
Vaison - la - Romaine

We returned to Malaucène & swam & had supper. The chocolate puddings were delicious.
photo_captions: Roman Bridge [CAD] over Ouvèze / Vaison - la - Romaine
pages.notes: Page number 58 visible in top-right corner. The page begins mid-sentence continuing from the left page. The top text (black ink) continues the description of the cathedral. A photograph is glued in showing a Roman bridge over a river gorge with a caption written in rotated hand: 'Roman Bridge [CAD] over Ouvèze / Vaison-la-Romaine'. The final paragraph is in blue ink (possibly a different pen or written at a different time). 'Hotel de la Beffroy' — diarist queries the origin of the word 'Beffroi' (belfry). 'CAD' in the photo caption is unclear; could be 'C.AD' (century AD) or an abbreviation.
